Research & Reviews: Journal of Agricultural Science & Technology

The Research & Reviews: Journal of Agricultural Science & Technology (RRJOAST) is a distinguished peer-reviewed publication dedicated to advancing scientific research, sustainable practices, and technological innovations in agriculture. This authoritative journal brings together agricultural scientists, agronomists, researchers, environmental specialists, policy makers, farming professionals, and agri-tech innovators to explore transformative insights shaping modern agriculture and global food security.

Covering crop science, soil management, agricultural biotechnology, farm mechanisation, plant protection, climate-smart agriculture, precision farming, and sustainable resource management, the journal publishes high-quality, peer-reviewed studies supporting the evolution of efficient, productive, and resilient agricultural systems. For professionals seeking to keep pace with global shifts in farming technologies, environmental standards, and agricultural productivity strategies, this journal serves as a comprehensive and credible knowledge resource.

What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

The Research & Reviews: Journal of Agricultural Science & Technology addresses a broad spectrum of essential and emerging themes in contemporary agriculture, including:

  • Crop Science & Plant Production: Crop improvement, plant breeding, seed technology, genetic enhancement, yield optimisation, cropping patterns, and sustainable cultivation practices.
  • Soil Science & Land Management: Soil fertility, nutrient management, soil health assessment, land-use planning, erosion control, conservation practices, and soil plant interactions.
  • Agricultural Biotechnology & Genomics: Transgenic crops, molecular breeding, genetic engineering, biofertilizers, biopesticides, microbial technology, and DNA-based diagnostic tools.
  • Farm Machinery & Precision Agriculture: Mechanisation systems, smart farming tools, drones, GIS-based monitoring, automation technologies, and precision input management.
  • Plant Protection & Pest Management: Integrated pest management (IPM), plant disease control, weed management, biological controls, pesticide technologies, and plant health surveillance.

Who Should Read the Journal

This journal serves a wide and diverse network of agricultural professionals, stakeholders, and scholars:

  • Agricultural scientists and agronomists are committed to improving crop productivity and sustainable farming methods.

  • Researchers and academicians are studying crop genetics, soil science, plant protection, and agricultural technologies.

  • Farmers, agri-consultants, and field experts are implementing advanced agricultural practices and precision farming systems.

  • Policy makers and regulatory authorities are shaping agricultural standards, farm sustainability rules, and food security policies.

  • Agri-tech innovators and entrepreneurs are developing digital tools, smart devices, and new technologies for modern farming.

Why It Matters

As global challenges related to food security, climate change, and sustainable agriculture intensify, the Research & Reviews: Journal of Agricultural Science & Technology plays a vital role by:

  • Publishing cutting-edge agricultural innovations that enhance productivity and resource efficiency

  • Promoting climate-resilient, environmentally responsible farming practices

  • Encouraging scientific advancements in biotechnology, crop science, and farm automation

  • Supporting sustainable agricultural development for improved rural and global livelihoods

  • Fostering interdisciplinary collaboration among agricultural science, environmental management, and technology-driven farming

With rigorous peer review, scientific credibility, and global relevance, the journal contributes to building a future-ready, sustainable, and technologically advanced agricultural ecosystem.

Subscription & Access Options

The Research & Reviews: Journal of Agricultural Science & Technology offers flexible access options suited for universities, research institutions, agricultural organisations, and individual professionals.

  • Digital Access: A comprehensive online platform offering instant access to current issues, archived volumes, and a fully searchable database of peer-reviewed agricultural research.

International Journal of Mechanical Dynamics and Systems Analysis

The International Journal of Mechanical Dynamics and Systems Analysis (IJMDSA) is a leading peer-reviewed journal focused on mechanical dynamics, vibration analysis, system modelling, control dynamics, and the behaviour of mechanical systems under various operating conditions. Bringing together experts in mechanical, aerospace, automotive, structural, and robotics engineering, the journal publishes high-impact research on vibration control, rotor dynamics, nonlinear and smart systems, and real-time monitoring, providing a vital platform for advancing performance, reliability, and innovation in dynamic mechanical systems.

What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

The International Journal of Mechanical Dynamics and Systems Analysis explores a wide spectrum of advanced and interdisciplinary research related to mechanical behaviour and system dynamics, including:

  • Dynamic System Modelling & Analysis – Linear and nonlinear system modelling, dynamic response evaluation, mechanical system behaviour, and analytical–numerical analysis.

  • Vibration Engineering & Control – Structural vibrations, machine vibrations, damping solutions, active and passive vibration control, and real-time vibration mitigation.

  • Rotor Dynamics & Machinery Health – Rotating machinery behaviour, imbalance and misalignment studies, stability analysis, condition monitoring, and fault diagnostics.

  • Nonlinear Dynamics & Chaos Theory – Nonlinear behaviours, bifurcation analysis, dynamic instability, chaos modelling, and complex dynamic responses in engineering systems.

International Journal of Fracture Mechanics and Damage Science 

The International Journal of Fracture Mechanics and Damage Science (IJFMDS) is a leading peer-reviewed publication focused on advancing research in fracture mechanics, damage evolution, material degradation, and structural failure analysis. Bringing together experts from mechanical engineering, materials science, civil engineering, aerospace, and computational modelling, the journal provides a specialized platform for understanding and preventing material failure.

What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

The International Journal of Fracture Mechanics and Damage Science explores a wide spectrum of advanced and interdisciplinary research related to material failure and structural integrity, including:

  • Fracture Mechanics & Crack Propagation – Linear and nonlinear fracture theories, crack initiation, crack growth behaviour, and predictive failure assessment.

  • Fatigue & Damage Evolution – Low-cycle and high-cycle fatigue behaviour, cumulative damage modelling, durability prediction, and material life assessment.

  • Computational Modelling & Simulation – FEM, XFEM, peridynamics, cohesive zone modelling, numerical simulation techniques, and digital fracture analysis.

  • Material Behaviour & Microstructural Damage – Study of metals, composites, polymers, ceramics, and advanced engineered materials under static, dynamic, thermal, and cyclic loading.

  • Structural Integrity & Failure Analysis – Real-world case studies, damage tolerance, impact and crash behaviour, fracture diagnostics, and structural health monitoring systems.

Who Should Read the Journal

International Journal of Data Structure Studies (IJDSS)

The International Journal of Data Structure Studies (IJDSS) is a respected peer-reviewed hybrid open-access journal dedicated to advancing research, innovation, and theoretical or applied developments in the field of data structures, algorithms, and related areas of computer science. This authoritative journal brings together computer scientists, software engineers, researchers, academicians, algorithm designers, data-management experts, and practitioners to share rigorous studies and transformative insights shaping the future of computing, data efficiency, and algorithmic design.

What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

The International Journal of Data Structure Studies addresses a broad range of critical and emerging topics across data structures, algorithms, and computational theory, including:

  • Fundamental & Classical Data Structures: Arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, basic data organization and management methods.

  • Advanced Data Structures & Memory-Efficient Designs: Trees (balanced/unbalanced), B-trees, suffix arrays, tries, hash-based structures, persistent data structures, and external-memory (disk/SSD-aware) data designs.

  • Algorithms & Complexity Analysis: Design and analysis of algorithms, recursive and dynamic algorithms, complexity studies, performance evaluation, optimization, and resource-aware algorithm design.

  • Parallel, Concurrent & External-Memory Data Structures: Data structures and algorithms optimized for parallelism, concurrency, external storage, high-volume data, large datasets, or distributed systems.

  • Language, Compiler & Theoretical Data Structure Studies: Data representation, memory layout, language-level data handling, compiler-level data abstractions, and theoretical investigations into data structure behavior and performance.

International Journal of Radio Frequency Design: Advancing RF Technologies, System Optimization, and Communication Innovations

The International Journal of Radio Frequency Design (IJRFD) is a peer-reviewed journal dedicated to advancing research and innovation in the rapidly evolving fields of radio frequency (RF) design, wireless communication systems, and electromagnetic technologies. The journal offers a platform for researchers, engineers, and professionals to explore cutting-edge developments in RF systems, including RF circuit design, antenna technologies, wireless communication, and next-generation communication systems.

IJRFD covers both theoretical and practical aspects of RF design, ensuring that the content is relevant for both academia and industry. It addresses key challenges in system optimization, signal integrity, and the design of high-performance RF components, providing valuable insights into improving the efficiency and reliability of RF systems.

What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

  • RF Circuit Design: Research on advanced RF components, low-noise amplifiers, mixers, oscillators, and filters used in communication systems.
  • Antenna Design and Optimization: In-depth studies on the design, simulation, and optimization of antennas for various communication applications, including mobile, satellite, and IoT.
  • Wireless Communication Systems: Design and optimization of RF systems in mobile, 5G/6G, and wireless IoT networks, with a focus on signal integrity, interference management, and frequency allocation.
  • Microwave Engineering: Insights into the design of microwave components and systems for high-frequency communication applications such as radar, satellite, and wireless communication.
  • RF Power Amplifiers and Transmitters: Research on the design and optimization of high-efficiency power amplifiers and transmitters to support the performance of modern RF systems.
  • Emerging RF Technologies: Exploration of novel RF techniques, including terahertz communication, photonics integration, software-defined radio (SDR), and quantum communication.
  • Telecommunication Standards and Policies: Articles focusing on RF spectrum management, regulatory frameworks, and the role of standards in shaping the global RF landscape.

Who Should Read the Journal

This publication is ideal for:

  • Engineers and researchers working in the field of RF design, wireless communication, and electromagnetic technologies.

  • Industry professionals involved in the development, design, and testing of RF components, circuits, and systems for telecommunications.
  • Telecommunications policymakers and regulatory bodies interested in RF spectrum management, communication standards, and global connectivity.
  • Innovators and technology developers exploring next-generation RF technologies and emerging communication paradigms.

Why It Matters

The International Journal of Radio Frequency Design plays a crucial role in the development of radio frequency systems, which are the backbone of modern telecommunications and wireless technologies. The journal is essential in:

  • Disseminating the latest research that supports advancements in RF systems and communication networks.
  • Addressing challenges such as interference reduction, signal distortion, and system optimization.

By focusing on both theoretical research and practical applications, the journal significantly contributes to shaping the future of RF system design and wireless communication technologies.

International Journal of Antibiotics

The International Journal of Antibiotics by STM Journals is a leading peer-reviewed publication focused on advanced research in antibiotic development, mechanisms of action, and antimicrobial resistance. It serves as a trusted resource for researchers, educators, nursing professionals, public-health experts, and students seeking reliable updates on modern antibiotic therapies and resistance management.

What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

Our journal explores a wide array of critical topics in the field of antibiotic discovery, resistance, and treatment. Key focus areas include:

  • Discovery and Development of New Antibiotics: Investigating novel antibiotics to combat emerging bacterial pathogens and address global health challenges.

  • Mechanisms of Antibiotic Resistance and Resistance Genes: Understanding how bacteria evolve resistance mechanisms and the genetic factors involved in resistance spread.

  • Antibiotic Pharmacology and Pharmacokinetics: Research on the absorption, distribution, metabolism, and excretion of antibiotics, optimizing their therapeutic use.

  • Clinical Trials and Therapeutic Applications of Antibiotics: Examining the clinical efficacy of antibiotics and exploring new therapeutic applications to fight infectious diseases.

  • Antimicrobial Stewardship and Resistance Mitigation Strategies: Developing strategies for appropriate antibiotic use, reducing resistance through stewardship, and promoting global health policies.

Each of these areas drives forward the science of antibiotic development and resistance mitigation, shaping the future of infectious disease treatment and public health.

Who Should Read This Journal

  • Healthcare professionals managing infectious diseases

  • Public health experts focusing on antibiotic resistance

Journal of Nuclear Engineering & Technology

The Journal of Nuclear Engineering & Technology (JONET) is a prestigious peer-reviewed publication committed to advancing scientific research, engineering innovation, and global understanding in the fields of nuclear engineering, reactor technology, nuclear safety, and advanced energy systems. This authoritative journal brings together academicians, nuclear scientists, reactor engineers, researchers, energy policymakers, radiation experts, and industry professionals to explore groundbreaking developments shaping the future of nuclear science and sustainable energy.

What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

The Journal of Nuclear Engineering & Technology addresses a broad range of essential and emerging themes in nuclear science and engineering, including:

  • Nuclear Reactor Design & Operation: Advanced reactor concepts, small modular reactors (SMRs), reactor physics, control systems, core design, neutron behavior, and reactor performance optimization.
  • Nuclear Materials & Fuel Technology: Fuel cycles, radioactive materials, corrosion resistance, structural materials, fuel fabrication, irradiation effects, and longevity of nuclear components.
  • Thermal Hydraulics & Heat Transfer: Coolant behavior, heat transport mechanisms, multiphase flow, thermal-fluid analysis, safety margins, and thermal performance studies.
  • Nuclear Safety, Risk & Regulation: Safety standards, failure analysis, reliability engineering, probabilistic risk assessment (PRA), emergency preparedness, and regulatory compliance.
  • Radiation Science & Protection: Radiation detection, shielding methods, radiological assessment, dosimetry, health physics, environmental impact, and exposure mitigation.

Journal of Constitutional Law and Jurisprudence

The Journal of Constitutional Law and Jurisprudence (JCLJ) is a distinguished peer-reviewed publication committed to advancing scholarship, legal doctrine, and practical understanding in the fields of constitutional law, comparative constitutional studies, and judicial reasoning. This authoritative academic platform unites scholars, jurists, legal practitioners, researchers, policy experts, and constitutional thinkers to share rigorous studies and transformative insights shaping the legal foundations of democratic governance and the rule of law.

Addressing constitutional interpretation, rights adjudication, judicial review, public law, constitutional design, federalism, and evolving jurisprudential debates, the journal publishes high-quality, peer-reviewed research that contributes to global discussions on constitutional governance and legal reform. For scholars and professionals seeking to remain informed about contemporary constitutional developments, judicial philosophies, and emerging legal standards, JCLJ offers a vital and trusted knowledge resource.

What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

The Journal of Constitutional Law and Jurisprudence explores a comprehensive range of essential and emerging areas within constitutional law, public governance, and judicial thought, including:

  • Constitutional Theory & Interpretation: Originalism, textualism, living constitutionalism, constitutional hermeneutics, interpretive methodologies, and philosophical foundations of constitutional adjudication.
  • Fundamental Rights & Liberties: Human rights jurisprudence, civil liberties, equality rights, constitutional protections, socio-economic rights, and evolving standards of rights enforcement.
  • Judicial Review & Constitutional Courts: Scope and limits of judicial review, constitutional court decisions, landmark judgments, institutional legitimacy, judicial activism/restraint, and doctrinal evolution.
  • Comparative Constitutional Law: Cross-jurisdictional constitutional analysis, global constitutional models, legal transplants, constitutional transitions, and comparative rights jurisprudence.
  • Governance, Institutions & Federalism: Separation of powers, checks and balances, parliamentary vs. presidential systems, distribution of governmental authority, federal–state relations, and constitutional accountability.

The Emerging Trends in Metabolites journal from STM Journals is a peer-reviewed academic publication dedicated to the latest advances in metabolite research and metabolomics. What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

This journal encompasses a comprehensive range of pivotal topics in metabolite research:

  • Metabolomics & Biomarker Discovery: Advanced analytical techniques (e.g., Mass Spectrometry, NMR) for identifying and quantifying metabolites as diagnostic, prognostic, and predictive biomarkers for diseases.

  • Metabolic Pathways & Systems Biology: Research on metabolic networks, flux analysis, and the integration of metabolomic data with genomics, transcriptomics, and proteomics for a holistic understanding of biology.

  • Nutritional Metabolomics & Food Science: Studies on the impact of diet on metabolism, biomarker discovery for nutritional status, and metabolite profiles of food for quality and safety assessment.

Who Should Read the Journal

It serves as a platform for researchers, engineers, and academics to explore the latest advancements in robotic systems, autonomous machinery, robotic mechanics, and industrial automation.

What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

The IJRAM covers a broad range of topics related to robotics and automation, ensuring its content is relevant for both academic research and industry development:

  • Robotic Systems and Control: In-depth research on robot design, motion control, kinematics, and dynamic modeling.

  • Autonomous Robotics: Exploring self-driving systems, autonomous machines, and AI-powered robots used in industries like manufacturing and healthcare.

  • Industrial Automation: Techniques in robotic automation for production lines, automated systems, and smart factories.

  • Mechatronics and System Integration: Studies on the integration of mechanical engineering, electronic systems, and computing for enhanced robot functionality.

  • Robot Perception and Sensing: Research on vision systems, sensor integration, and environmental perception for autonomous robots.

Who Should Read the Journal

This journal is ideal for:

  • Engineers and researchers in the fields of robotics, automation, mechanical engineering, and mechatronics.

  • Academics and students interested in the theory and applications of robotic systems and automation technologies.

  • Industry professionals focused on robotic systems design, automation solutions, and manufacturing technologies.

  • Policymakers and regulatory bodies interested in the role of robotics and automation in future industries.

  • Innovators and technology developers seeking to advance the capabilities of autonomous machinery.

Why It Matters

The International Journal of Robotics and Automation in Mechanics (IJRAM) plays a pivotal role in the future of robotics and mechanical automation. The journal is vital in:

  • Disseminating the latest research that drives robotic advancements and automation technologies.

  • Addressing challenges such as robot autonomy, mechanical integration, and human-robot interaction.

  • Immunopathology & Disease Mechanisms: Autoimmune diseases, inflammatory disorders and molecular mechanisms underlying immune-related diseases.
  • Infectious Disease Immunology: Immune response to viral, bacterial and fungal infections, vaccine response analysis; and global infectious disease immunity studies.
  • Vaccine Science & Immunization Strategies: Vaccine development, adjuvant technologies, immune memory, booster research, and next-generation vaccine platforms.
  • Immunodiagnostics & Clinical Immunology: Diagnostic biomarkers, serology, point-of-care immunoassays, immune profiling, clinical evaluation of immunotherapies, and precision immunology.

Research & Reviews: A Journal of Bioinformatics

Research & Reviews: A Journal of Bioinformatics (RRJOBI) is a peer-reviewed, hybrid open-access journal dedicated to advancing innovation and scholarly research at the intersection of biology, computer science, and statistics. Launched in 2014 and published tri-annually, RRJoBI provides a global platform for researchers, computational biologists, bioinformaticians, data scientists, academicians, and industry professionals to share high-quality original research, review articles, methodological advances, and applied studies in the rapidly evolving field of bioinformatics.

What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

RRJoBI publishes original research across a broad spectrum of bioinformatics and computational biology, including but not limited to:

  • Sequence Analysis & Database Mining — Sequence alignment, sequence mining and profiling, genome and transcriptome assembly, clustering, mapping, and sequence database research.

  • Systems Biology & Biological Networks — Systems-level analyses, biological networks, protein–protein interaction networks, signaling pathways, network modeling, and systems-level computational biology.

  • Phylogeny & Evolutionary Algorithms — Phylogeny reconstruction, evolutionary computation, comparative genomics, and algorithmic tools for evolutionary biology.

  • Structural Bioinformatics & Protein Modeling — Protein structure prediction, computational modeling, in-silico prediction tools, structural biology analyses, and computational proteomics.

  • Computational Genomics, Transcriptomics & Omics Data Analysis — Genomic data analysis, transcriptome sequencing pipelines, omics data mining, big-data analytics, and statistical bioinformatics methods.

Who Should Read / Use RRJoBI?

RRJoBI is ideal for:

  • Researchers, academicians, and students in bioinformatics, computational biology, genomics, proteomics, systems biology, and related life-science disciplines.

  • Data scientists, computational biologists & bioinformaticians working on sequence analysis, omics data, structural modeling, and algorithm development.

  • Industry professionals in biotech, genomics, drug discovery, computational biology, and data-driven research environments.

  • Educators and academic institutions teaching bioinformatics, computational biology, genomics, and statistical biology.

  • Interdisciplinary collaborators bridging biology, computer science, statistics, and data science to solve complex biological problems with computational methods.

Why RRJoBI Matters

In an era dominated by big-data biology, genomics breakthroughs, and computational approaches — RRJoBI plays a vital role by:

  • Publishing peer-reviewed, high-impact research that advances computational biology, genomics, structural bioinformatics, and systems biology.

  • Fostering interdisciplinary collaboration between biology, computer science, and statistics — enabling novel computational solutions to biological challenges.

  • Offering global visibility and wide indexing — allowing researchers from around the world to access cutting-edge bioinformatics studies.

  • Providing a diverse and comprehensive resource — supporting sequence analysis, omics data interpretation, algorithm development, structural modeling, and systems-level biology research.

Published by STM Journals, the International Journal of Architectural Heritage (IJAH) stands as a distinguished peer-reviewed platform dedicated to the study, preservation, restoration, and management of architectural heritage across the globe. This authoritative scholarly journal unites conservation experts, architects, historians, researchers, engineers, cultural heritage professionals, and policy specialists to share transformative research shaping the future of built heritage protection.

 What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

The International Journal of Architectural Heritage (ijah) spans a wide range of heritage-focused and interdisciplinary topics, including:

  • Heritage Conservation & Restoration: Methodologies for conserving historic buildings, monuments, archaeological sites, cultural landmarks, and urban heritage environments.
  • Material Science & Preservation Technologies: Advanced studies on traditional building materials, deterioration processes, conservation treatments, restoration technologies, and compatibility of repair materials.
  • Structural Analysis & Conservation Engineering: Assessment of structural integrity in historical constructions, seismic vulnerability studies, retrofitting techniques, and structural reinforcement strategies.
  • Cultural Landscapes & Historic Urban Environments: Research on heritage cities, cultural landscapes, urban morphology, traditional settlements, and the integration of heritage into contemporary planning.
  • Digital Heritage & Documentation: Digital recording techniques including photogrammetry, 3D scanning, BIM for heritage, virtual reconstruction, and tech-driven documentation methodologies.

Who Should Read the Journal

This journal serves as a valuable reference for a diverse spectrum of heritage and architectural professionals:

  • Conservation architects and restoration specialists are preserving historic structures and cultural sites.

  • Architectural historians and researchers are studying cultural evolution, traditional craftsmanship, and built heritage significance.

  • Structural and civil engineers working on restoration, seismic assessment, and strengthening of heritage buildings.

  • Urban planners and cultural heritage managers are integrating historical assets into modern planning frameworks.

  • Archaeologists and heritage scientists are analysing materials, deterioration, and conservation treatments.

Why It Matters

As heritage conservation becomes increasingly vital in a rapidly changing world, the International Journal of Architectural Heritage drives global progress by:

  • Publishing high-impact research that strengthens conservation methodologies

  • Showcasing innovative restoration technologies and sustainable preservation practices

  • Supporting international heritage management, policy development, and cultural protection

  • Enhancing understanding of historical architecture across eras and regions

  • Facilitating interdisciplinary collaboration among architects, engineers, historians, and conservation scientists

Through rigorous peer review, global knowledge exchange, and a commitment to cultural stewardship, the journal advances the preservation of architectural heritage worldwide while promoting responsible and sustainable conservation.

Subscription & Access Option

The International Journal of Architectural Heritage provides flexible and globally accessible formats for readers and institutions:

  • Online Subscription: Access full issues, archives, and research articles anytime through STM Journals’ digital platform, designed for user convenience and global reach.
  • Print Subscription: Receive physical copies delivered directly to institutions or homes, ideal for study, teaching, and reference purposes.

Subscription plans, institutional access options, and volume orders can be arranged through the STM Journals distribution team and support services.

STM Journals ensures excellence in scholarly publishing through:

  • Comprehensive peer review guaranteeing research integrity and scientific credibility

  • Global accessibility connecting heritage researchers and professionals across cultures and continents

  • Multiple reading formats supporting diverse academic, research, and educational needs

  • A dedicated focus on advancing architectural conservation and heritage studies

For researchers, conservation experts, and cultural heritage advocates, the International Journal of Architectural Heritage serves as a trusted, authoritative, and globally relevant research platform.

International Journal of Chemical Separation Technology

The International Journal of Chemical Separation Technology (IJCST) is a leading peer-reviewed journal dedicated to advancing the science, engineering, and industrial application of chemical separation processes. This authoritative publication brings together chemical engineers, process technologists, researchers, laboratory scientists, industrial professionals, and policymakers to share high-impact research shaping the future of separation science and process engineering.

What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

The International Journal of Chemical Separation Technology explores a wide range of critical and emerging topics in chemical engineering and separation science, including:

  • Membrane Separation & Advanced Filtration: Ultrafiltration, nanofiltration, microfiltration, reverse osmosis, membrane fouling control, hybrid membrane systems, and solvent-resistant membranes.
  • Distillation, Absorption & Extraction: Advanced distillation, reactive distillation, azeotropic separation, absorption processes, liquid–liquid extraction, solvent extraction, and separation of complex mixtures.
  • Adsorption, Ion Exchange & Chromatography: Adsorbent materials, activated carbon, zeolites, ion-exchange resins, chromatographic separation, surface chemistry, and breakthrough curve analysis.
  • Separation Process Design & Optimization: Process simulation, modeling and computational analysis, energy efficiency, process intensification, dynamic separation systems, and hybrid separation technologies.
  • Environmental & Green Separation Technologies: Wastewater treatment, pollutant removal, recovery of valuable compounds, emission reduction technologies, zero-liquid-discharge systems, and sustainable chemical processing.

Who Should Read the Journal

The journal is essential for a broad community of scientific and engineering professionals:

  • Chemical engineers work on process design, separation systems, and industrial purification challenges.

  • Industrial professionals in the petrochemical, pharmaceutical, environmental, and manufacturing sectors are focusing on efficient separation processes.

  • Environmental and wastewater engineers implementing separation-based treatment solutions.

  • Academics and researchers studying membrane technology, adsorption, distillation, and process optimization.

  • Policy makers and regulators are shaping guidelines for emissions control, environmental compliance, and industrial processing standards.

Why It Matters

As industries worldwide seek more sustainable, energy-efficient, and cost-effective separation solutions, the International Journal of Chemical Separation Technology plays a vital role by:

  • Publishing cutting-edge research that advances separation science and process engineering

  • Supporting the development of green, low-energy, and environmentally responsible technologies

  • Enhancing industrial efficiency through innovative separation and purification methods

  • Guiding researchers and engineers in solving complex chemical processing challenges

  • Fostering collaboration across chemical engineering, materials science, biotechnology, and environmental engineering

Through rigorous peer review, scientific integrity, and global knowledge exchange, IJCST contributes to innovation, sustainability, and technological advancement in chemical separation processes worldwide.

International Journal of Green Chemistry (IJGC)

The International Journal of Green Chemistry (IJGC) is a peer-reviewed platform dedicated to advancing the development and application of green chemistry principles. What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

The IJGC addresses a diverse array of topics in the realm of green chemistry, ensuring its relevance to both academic research and practical industry needs:

  • Green Catalysis: Research on environmentally benign catalysts that reduce the need for toxic chemicals and solvents in chemical reactions. This includes studies on biocatalysis, heterogeneous catalysts, and enzyme-based reactions.
  • Sustainable Chemical Processes: Innovations in process design and optimization aimed at reducing waste, energy consumption, and the carbon footprint of chemical manufacturing.
  • Environmental Impact Reduction: Techniques to reduce emissions, improve energy efficiency, and enhance recycling in chemical industries, addressing global challenges like climate change and resource depletion.
  • Waste Minimization and Recycling: Research on the development of closed-loop systems, waste recycling technologies, and methods for reusing by-products to reduce environmental pollution.
  • Green Chemistry in Industrial Applications: Case studies and industrial applications of green chemistry in sectors like pharmaceuticals, agriculture, materials science, and manufacturing.

This comprehensive journal connects environmental chemists, toxicologists, analytical scientists, environmental engineers, policy experts, industry professionals, and researchers who are shaping the future of environmental protection and chemical sustainability.

What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

  • Environmental Pollutants & Contaminant Chemistry: Chemical fate and transport, persistent organic pollutants, heavy metals, pesticides, microplastics, industrial waste chemicals, and emerging contaminants.
  • Water, Soil & Air Chemistry: Surface and groundwater chemistry, soil contamination analysis, atmospheric reactions, aerosol chemistry, chemical deposition, nutrient cycles, and environmental monitoring.
  • Analytical & Instrumental Techniques: Advanced analytical methods such as chromatography, spectroscopy, mass spectrometry, electrochemical techniques, biosensors, rapid detection systems, and environmental sampling protocols.
  • Environmental Toxicology & Risk Assessment: Toxic effects of chemicals on ecosystems and human health, dose–response analysis, ecotoxicity modeling, biodegradation, and long-term exposure risks.

aboutJournal:
International Journal of Agrochemistry

The International Journal of Agrochemistry (IJA) is a peer-reviewed journal dedicated to advancing the research and application of agrochemicals in agriculture, food production, and environmental protection. It serves as a comprehensive platform for scientists, agronomists, researchers, and professionals to explore the latest trends, innovations, and practices in agrochemical science, offering cutting-edge studies on the use of fertilizers, pesticides, herbicides, and growth regulators in agriculture.

IJAC provides crucial insights into the development and safe use of agrochemicals for increasing agricultural productivity while ensuring environmental sustainability. It addresses both the scientific and practical aspects of agrochemical applications, focusing on optimizing crop yield, pest and disease management, soil health, and sustainable farming practices.

What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

The IJAC covers a broad spectrum of critical and emerging topics in agrochemistry, ensuring its content remains relevant for both academic research and real-world applications:

  • Agrochemical Applications in Crop Production: Studies on the development and use of pesticides, herbicides, and fungicides for efficient crop protection, pest management, and yield improvement.

  • Fertilizer Chemistry and Soil Health: Research on fertilizers, soil nutrients, and sustainable fertilization practices that enhance soil quality, nutrient availability, and long-term crop productivity.

  • Environmental Impact of Agrochemicals: Investigations into the ecological effects of agrochemical use, including soil contamination, water pollution, and biodiversity loss, as well as strategies for mitigating these impacts.

  • Biological Control and Natural Pesticides: Research on bio-based agrochemicals and sustainable alternatives to synthetic pesticides, such as microbial agents, biocontrol mechanisms, and plant-derived pesticides.

  • Regulatory and Safety Aspects of Agrochemicals: Articles on safety standards, regulatory frameworks, and best practices for the safe application of agrochemicals in agriculture, including compliance with environmental and health regulations.

The International Journal of Microwave Engineering and Technology (IJMET) is a leading peer-reviewed scientific journal dedicated to advancing research, innovation, and practical applications in microwave engineering, RF technologies, and high-frequency system design.   • RF & Microwave Circuit Design: RFICs, MMICs, frequency synthesizers, low-noise amplifiers (LNAs), power amplifiers, microwave subsystems, and integrated microwave circuit technologies.
  • Antenna Design & Electromagnetic Radiation: Antenna modeling, phased arrays, smart antennas, microstrip antennas, MIMO systems, beamforming, radiation patterns, and electromagnetic wave propagation studies.

Published by STM Journals, the International Journal of Obstetrics, Perinatal and Neonatal Nursing (IJOPNN) is a leading peer-reviewed scholarly platform dedicated to advancing research, best practices, and innovations in maternal, perinatal, and neonatal nursing care. This journal brings together midwives, obstetric nurses, neonatologists, maternal-child health researchers, pediatric nurses, healthcare policy experts, and allied health professionals to share evidence-based studies and transformative insights shaping the future of perinatal and neonatal health care.

 What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

The International Journal of Obstetrics, Perinatal and Neonatal Nursing encompasses a broad and vital range of topics central to maternal and newborn health, including:

  • Pregnancy & Antenatal Care: Studies on prenatal care practices, maternal nutrition, risk assessment, pregnancy complications, health education for expectant mothers, and psychosocial support strategies.
  • Childbirth, Labor & Delivery Nursing: Research on labor management, midwifery practices, pain management, birthing techniques, maternal comfort, safe delivery protocols, and personalized care during childbirth.
  • Perinatal & Postnatal Care: Maternal postpartum care, mother-infant bonding, breastfeeding support, mental health during the postpartum period, lactation nursing, and perinatal education.
  • Neonatal Nursing & Newborn Care: Neonatal intensive care, preterm care, newborn screening, early life interventions, neonatal morbidity and mortality studies, neonatal nutrition, infection prevention, and developmental support for neonates.
  • Family-Centered & Community-Based Care: Parental education, community health outreach, home-based maternal and child care programs, family-centered neonatal care, and culturally sensitive healthcare practices.

International Journal of Antibiotics

The International Journal of Antibiotics by STM Journals is a leading peer-reviewed publication focused on advanced research in antibiotic development, mechanisms of action, and antimicrobial resistance. It serves as a trusted resource for researchers, educators, nursing professionals, public-health experts, and students seeking reliable updates on modern antibiotic therapies and resistance management.

What This Journal Covers: Key Focus Areas

Our journal explores a wide array of critical topics in the field of antibiotic discovery, resistance, and treatment. Key focus areas include:

  • Discovery and Development of New Antibiotics: Investigating novel antibiotics to combat emerging bacterial pathogens and address global health challenges.

  • Mechanisms of Antibiotic Resistance and Resistance Genes: Understanding how bacteria evolve resistance mechanisms and the genetic factors involved in resistance spread.

  • Antibiotic Pharmacology and Pharmacokinetics: Research on the absorption, distribution, metabolism, and excretion of antibiotics, optimizing their therapeutic use.

  • Clinical Trials and Therapeutic Applications of Antibiotics: Examining the clinical efficacy of antibiotics and exploring new therapeutic applications to fight infectious diseases.

  • Antimicrobial Stewardship and Resistance Mitigation Strategies: Developing strategies for appropriate antibiotic use, reducing resistance through stewardship, and promoting global health policies.

Each of these areas drives forward the science of antibiotic development and resistance mitigation, shaping the future of infectious disease treatment and public health.
